2605.05104.title: Building informative materials datasets beyond targeted objectives
2605.05104.paper_summary: Materials science data collection can be expensive, making the reuse and long-term utility of datasets critical important for future discovery campaigns. In practice, researchers prioritize a subset of properties due to research interests. However, ignoring a subset of outcomes in data collection campaigns potentially generate datasets poorly suited for future learning tasks. Here, we present a framework for dataset construction that maximizes informativeness for target properties of interest while preserving performance on untargeted ones. Our approach uses diversity-aware selection to ensure broad coverage of the materials space. In noisy experimental dataset construction, we find that without our diversity-aware framework, prediction performance on untargeted properties can degrade by up to 40% relative to random sampling, whereas applying our framework yields improvements of up to 10% . For targeted properties, performance can degrade with respect to random sampling by up to 12.5% without diversity, while our framework achieves gains of up to 25%. Incorporating diversity into dataset construction not only preserves informativeness for the targeted properties, but also improves materials coverage for potential future objectives. As a result, the constructed datasets remain broadly informative across considered and unconsidered outcomes, ensuring unbiased quality entries and mitigating cold-start limitations in subsequent modeling and discovery campaigns.
2605.05104.paper_summary_zh: 材料科學數據收集可能成本高昂，使得數據集的重用和長期效用對於未來的發現活動至關重要。<lb/>在實踐中，研究人員根據研究興趣優先考慮一部分屬性。<lb/>然而，在數據收集活動中忽略某些結果可能會產生不適合未來學習任務的數據集。<lb/>在此，我們提出了一個數據集構建框架，旨在最大化對目標屬性的資訊量，同時保持對非目標屬性的性能。<lb/>我們的方法使用考慮多樣性的選擇，以確保材料空間的廣泛覆蓋。<lb/>在噪聲實驗數據集構建中，我們發現如果沒有我們的考慮多樣性的框架，對非目標屬性的預測性能可能相對於隨機抽樣下降多達40%，而應用我們的框架則可提高最多10%。<lb/>對於目標屬性，若不考慮多樣性，性能相對於隨機抽樣可能下降多達12.5%，而我們的框架則可實現最多25%的增益。<lb/>將多樣性納入數據集構建不僅保留了對目標屬性的資訊量，還改善了對潛在未來目標的材料覆蓋。因此，所構建的數據集在考慮和未考慮的結果之間保持廣泛的資訊量，確保無偏的質量條目並減輕後續建模和發現活動中的冷啟動限制。
2605.05104.author: Rafael Espinosa Castañeda et.al.
2605.05104.authors: Rafael Espinosa Castañeda, Ashley Dale, Hongchen Wang, Yonatan Kurniawan, Hao Wan, Runze Zhang, Adji Bousso Dieng, Kangming Li, Jason Hattrick-Simpers
